title: Pleurothallis miniatura
taxonomy: Pleurothallis miniatura is classified in the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Pleurothallis is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which contains many species of orchids. Pleurothallis miniatura is further classified into the tribe Epidendreae, which includes over 1,600 species of orchids. The classification of Pleurothallis miniatura reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species. The precise classification of Pleurothallis miniatura is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of orchid taxonomy.

intro: Pleurothallis miniatura, a species of orchid native to Central and South America, is a type of epiphytic orchid known for its delicate and intricate floral structure. Traditional medicine has long valued Pleurothallis miniatura for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis miniatura include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Pleurothallis miniatura typically include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
